meta.title: GEM Dashboard - Global Equity Momentum Strategy Tracker
meta.description: Track Global Equity Momentum (GEM) investment signals in real-time. Momentum scores across strategies with historical analysis and allocation insights.

status.title: System Status
status.description: Real-time monitoring and data source information

strategy.title: About the Strategy
strategy.subtitle: How this strategy allocates each month

canonical.summary: Buys the stronger of {equity} and {intl} while {equity} outpaces {threshold}; otherwise holds {bond}.
canonical.whatIsDescription: Canonical Global Equity Momentum (GEM) anchors on {equity}. Each month it checks whether {equity}'s 12-month return beats {threshold} (the risk-free rate). If it does, it holds whichever of {equity} or {intl} has the stronger 12-month momentum; if not, it moves entirely to {bond} for downside protection.
canonical.step1: Calculate the 12-month total return for {equity}, {intl}, and {threshold}
canonical.step3: If yes, hold the stronger of {equity} vs {intl} (relative momentum)
canonical.step4: If no, move 100% to {bond} for capital preservation
canonical.step5: Rebalance monthly based on updated momentum signals

argmax.summary: Each month holds whichever of its four assets has the strongest 12-month momentum — cash is one of them, so it parks in cash when markets fall.
argmax.whatIsDescription: This strategy ranks all four assets — {assets} — by their 12-month return and holds the single top performer. Because short-term bonds (cash) are one of the four, a falling market naturally parks the allocation in cash without any separate rule.
argmax.step1: Calculate the 12-month total return for all assets: {assets}
argmax.step2: Rank them by momentum, highest first
argmax.step3: Hold 100% of the top performer (which can be {cash} when everything else is weak)
argmax.step4: Rebalance monthly based on updated momentum signals

strategy.resourceCraftsman: Global Equity Momentum: A Craftsman's Perspective

allocation.title: Current Allocation
allocation.interpretationEq1: {eq1} ({eq1Mom}) cleared the {threshold} gate ({thresholdMom}) and outpaced international stocks ({eq2Mom}). The strategy allocates 100% to {eq1}.
allocation.interpretationEq2: {eq1} ({eq1Mom}) cleared the {threshold} gate ({thresholdMom}), and international stocks ({eq2Mom}) had the stronger momentum. The strategy allocates 100% to {eq2}.
allocation.interpretationBond: {eq1} ({eq1Mom}) did not beat the {threshold} gate ({thresholdMom}). The strategy moves to bonds ({bondMom}) for capital preservation.
allocation.interpretationArgmax: {asset} has the highest 12-month momentum ({assetMom}) among the four assets. The strategy allocates 100% to it.

history.title: History Analysis
history.cardDescription: Chart and table show the same date range

allocationChanges.title: Allocation Changes
allocationChanges.stableHistory: Signal has been stable as {signal} for the entire available history ({days} days).
allocationChanges.loadingError: Failed to load allocation change data.

note.greeting: Hey 👋 I'm Krzysztof, and I built this dashboard because I run a slice of my own savings on Global Equity Momentum and got tired of recomputing the signal by hand every month.
note.para2: The trackers I found either sat behind a paywall or buried the actual rule under charts I didn't need. I just wanted one page I could open, see this month's allocation, and close. So I built it.
note.para3: My hope is that it makes the strategy a little less of a black box for you too.
note.disclaimer: None of this is investment advice — it's a personal tool for tracking a public, rules-based strategy. Do your own research, and remember that past momentum guarantees nothing about the future.
note.ps: (PS you can find the rest of my work at <site>kristof.pro</site>, or the occasional note on <substack>Substack</substack>.)
